content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
'>Practices Commission 
Gerard C. Danna 
Special Investigator 
July 17, 1987 
Massachusetts State Ethics Commission 
McCormack state Office Building, Rm. 619 
One Ashburton Place 
Boston, MA 
02108 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. 1-87-156 
Dear Mr. Danna: 
Your letter of May 29, 1987, to Secretary of State March 
Fong Eu has been referred to this office for response. 
I am 
forwarding a copy of the Political Reform Act and the 
commission's regulations on gifts (2 Cal. Adm. Code section 
18726-18726.8) with this letter. 
I hope they are useful. 
I am 
not certain what else, if anything, you may be interested in. 
If we can be of further assistance, please let me know. 
DMG:km 
Enclosures 
Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916) 322,5660

The Commonwealth of Massachusetts

State Ethics Commission 
secretary of state's Office 
Political Reform Division 
1230 J street 
Sacramento, CA 
95814: 
HI PI,!" 
B(hlOrl 
) lOll 
)nt' i(,l~ "2 ()()(,() 
May 29, 1987 
Attn: 
March Long Fu, Program Mgr. and Secretary of State 
Dear Mr. Fu: 
Our office is presently involved in a number of 
investigations involving government officials who are receiving 
gratuities from bank officials with whom they conduct government 
business. 
We are presently soliciting information from other ethics 
commissions around the nation pertaining to similar issues. 
If your office has issued any advisories or adjudicatory 
decisions or if there is any relevant case law from your state 
court system, we would appreciate you forwarding copies to us. 
We additionally would appreciate a copy of any relevant sections 
of your statute. 
We are most concerned with the issues of ·substantial value* 
and the use of accrual of gratuities for enforcement purposes, as 
well as the defense of Hbusiness justificationH. 
Your assistance in this matter will be greatly appreciated. 
If you have any questions please contact Attorney Robert Levite 
or Special Investigator Gerard Danna at (617)727-0060. 
GCD:ph 
Sincerely, 
Gerard C. Danna 
Special Investigator

sections.question: Our office is presently involved in a number of investigations involving government officials who are receiving gratuities from bank officials with whom they conduct government business. We are presently soliciting information from other ethics commissions around the nation pertaining to similar issues. If your office has issued any advisories or adjudicatory decisions or if there is any relevant case law from your state court system, we would appreciate you forwarding copies to us. We additionally would appreciate a copy of any relevant sections of your statute. We are most concerned with the issues of 'substantial value' and the use of accrual of gratuities for enforcement purposes, as well as the defense of 'business justification'.
sections.conclusion: I am forwarding a copy of the Political Reform Act and the commission's regulations on gifts (2 Cal. Adm. Code section 18726-18726.8) with this letter. I hope they are useful. I am not certain what else, if anything, you may be interested in. If we can be of further assistance, please let me know.
sections.question_synthetic: The Massachusetts State Ethics Commission requests information from California's FPPC regarding advisories, decisions, and case law concerning gratuities received by government officials, with particular focus on 'substantial value,' accumulation of gratuities for enforcement, and 'business justification' defenses.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC provided copies of the Political Reform Act and gift regulations to the requesting Massachusetts commission, indicating uncertainty about additional assistance available.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (dated May 29, 1987) and the FPPC response letter (dated July 17, 1987). Response is minimal and does not provide substantive legal analysis. File No. 1-87-156 matches the document ID provided.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Our office is presently involved in a number of investigations involving government officials who are receiving gratuities from bank officials with whom they conduct government business. We are presently soliciting information from other ethics commissions around the nation pertaining to similar issues. If your office has issued any advisories or adjudicatory decisions or if there is any relevant case law from your state court system, we would appreciate you forwarding copies to us. We additionally would appreciate a copy of any relevant sections of your statute. We are most concerned with the issues of 'substantial value' and the use of accrual of gratuities for enforcement purposes, as well as the defense of 'business justification'.

CONCLUSION: I am forwarding a copy of the Political Reform Act and the commission's regulations on gifts (2 Cal. Adm. Code section 18726-18726.8) with this letter. I hope they are useful. I am not certain what else, if anything, you may be interested in. If we can be of further assistance, please let me know.

embedding.summary: The Massachusetts State Ethics Commission requested information from California's FPPC on gift gratuity issues, and the FPPC responded by providing copies of California's Political Reform Act and gift regulations without additional analysis or advisory guidance.
